what: Why the adjustments matter for variance
title: Variance estimation
text: weighting recipe rarely stops design weight. redistributes unknown eligibility, drops --scope units, adjusts nonresponse calibrates known totals. stages estimated sample, one adds (, calibration, often removes) variability. linearization takes final weights fixed applies ultimate-cluster formula ignores nonresponse calibration steps estimated. cleanest way account re-run whole recipe replicate, replicate weights carry variability every stage.

what: Method 1: a PSU bootstrap that re-applies the recipe
title: Variance estimation
text: bootstrap_weights() resamples primary sampling units (PSUs) replacement within strata re-runs recipe replicate. Pass inert recipe (call prep() first): bootstrap preps per replicate. multiplier Rao-Wu rescaling bootstrap. Within stratum n PSUs, m PSUs drawn replacement (default m = n - 1), unit whose PSU drawn t times rescaled expectation one never turns negative. Whole PSUs kept together (every unit drawn PSU retained), design’s clustering requires.
code: dat <- sample_one dat$age_grp <- cut(dat$age, c(0, 30, 45, 60, Inf),                    labels = c("18-30", "31-45", "46-60", "60+"))  spec <- weighting_spec(dat, base_weights = pw) |>   step_unknown_eligibility(unknown = unknown_elig, by = "region") |>   step_drop_ineligible(ineligible = ineligible) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = hh_responded, method = "weighting_class",                    by = "region") |>   step_select_within(prob = p_within) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class",                    by = c("region", "sex", "age_grp")) |>   step_calibrate(method = "raking",                  margins = list(region = c(table(population$region)),                                 sex    = c(table(population$sex))))  boot <- bootstrap_weights(spec, replicates = 200, strata = "region",                           psu = "psu", seed = 2024, progress = FALSE) boot #> <weightflow bootstrap> #>   replicates : 200 #>   units      : 417 (active: 209) #>   strata     : region #>   psu        : psu lambda = 1 - sqrt(m / (n - 1)) + sqrt(m / (n - 1)) * (n / m) * t

previous_headings: Method 1: a PSU bootstrap that re-applies the recipe
title: Variance estimation
text: bootstrap variance (1 / B) * sum((theta_b - theta_hat)^2). statistic, pass function weights data bootstrap_estimate():
code: boot_mean(boot,  "income")     # mean income #>   estimate       se ci_lower ci_upper #> 1 21615.21 872.7788 19904.59 23325.82 boot_total(boot, "employed")   # total employed #>   estimate       se ci_lower ci_upper #> 1 1927.219 140.9421 1650.978 2203.461 boot_mean(boot,  "employed")   # employment rate #>    estimate         se  ci_lower  ci_upper #> 1 0.4287473 0.03102821 0.3679331 0.4895615 bootstrap_estimate(boot, function(w, d) {   ok <- !is.na(d$income) & w > 0   stats::median(rep(d$income[ok], times = round(w[ok])))   # weighted median (approx.) }) #>   estimate     se ci_lower ci_upper #> 1    18136 930.15 16312.94 19959.06

what: Method 2: hand the weights to the survey package
title: Variance estimation
text: as_svydesign() builds ultimate-cluster linearization design prepped recipe. fast, treats calibration fixed. keep recipe’s adjustments variance still using survey, feed bootstrap replicate weights method 1: matches boot_mean(boot, "income") exactly, as_svrepdesign() sets scale = 1 / B, rscales = 1 mse = TRUE.
code: fitted <- prep(spec) des <- as_svydesign(fitted, ids = "psu", strata = "region") survey::svymean(~income, des, na.rm = TRUE) #>         mean     SE #> income 21615 989.34 rep_des <- as_svrepdesign(boot) survey::svymean(~income, rep_des, na.rm = TRUE) #>         mean     SE #> income 21615 872.78

what: Replicate weights for a tidyverse workflow
title: Variance estimation
text: collect_replicate_weights() attaches point weight (.weight) replicate weights (rep_1 … rep_B) active respondents, ready srvyr.
code: df <- collect_replicate_weights(boot) d_rep <- srvyr::as_survey_rep(df, weights = .weight,                               repweights = dplyr::starts_with("rep_"),                               type = "bootstrap", combined.weights = TRUE,                               scale = 1 / attr(df, "R"), rscales = 1, mse = TRUE) srvyr::summarise(d_rep, mean_income = srvyr::survey_mean(income, na.rm = TRUE)) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   mean_income mean_income_se #>         <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1      21615.           873.

title: Variance estimation
text: Use recipe-aware bootstrap (method 1, three forms) nonresponse calibration steps meaningful part design want uncertainty reflected; honest variance. Use linearization (method 2) quick, well-understood standard error adjustments minor need design--clustering part. practical notes. replicates give stable bootstrap SE; 200 fine exploration, 500-1000 final figures. stratum needs least two PSUs resampled (single-PSU strata left untouched, warning). replicate leaves calibration weighting-class cell empty dropped warning; coarser cells make bootstrap robust.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Juan Pablo Ferreira. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ferreira J (2026). weightflow: Declarative API Staged Survey Weights. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/jpferreira33/weightflow.
code: @Manual{,   title = {weightflow: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights},   author = {Juan Pablo Ferreira},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/jpferreira33/weightflow}, }

what: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
title: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
text: Declarative, pipeable survey weighting base R — design weights calibrated, variance-ready weights. weightflow builds survey weights chaining hierarchical adjustments tidymodels-style API, estimates variances bootstrap re-applies whole recipe replicate. hard dependencies (base R, R >= 4.1) bridges survey/srvyr design-based inference.

title: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("jpferreira33/weightflow")

title: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
text: recipe inert: building computes nothing. prep() walks steps order estimates cascade factors; collect_weights() extracts final weights. Separating define apply makes whole process reproducible auditable, exactly lets bootstrap re-run entire cascade per replicate.
code: library(weightflow)  recipe <- weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_unknown_eligibility(unknown = unknown_elig, by = "region") |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class",                    by = c("region", "sex")) |>   step_calibrate(method = "raking",                  margins = list(region = c(table(population$region)),                                 sex    = c(table(population$sex))))  fitted <- prep(recipe)              # estimate the cascade summary(fitted)                     # per-stage diagnostics + Kish deff wts    <- collect_weights(fitted)   # data.frame with .weight

title: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
text: Adjustment steps, applied order pipe : Eligibility response accept 0/1 dummy columns logical condition. Diagnostics reporting: summary() plot() show per-stage cascade Kish design effect (deff = 1 + CV²) effective sample size; weight_factors() returns per-unit, per-step factors; report_weighting() writes self-contained HTML report — pipeline diagram, variables used, per-stage summaries per-step visuals — graphics device server required. Variance estimation (see Variance estimation article): bootstrap resamples PSUs within strata (Rao-Wu rescaling bootstrap) re-applies recipe replicate, replicate weights carry variability every adjustment.
code: boot <- bootstrap_weights(recipe, replicates = 500, strata = "region", psu = "psu") boot_mean(boot, "income")           # estimate, SE and CI as_svydesign(fitted, ids = "psu", strata = "region")   # survey linearization collect_replicate_weights(boot)     # replicate weights, ready for srvyr

title: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
text: Three bundled datasets: population (frame), sample_survey (take-roster) sample_one (multistage select-one design), stratum, PSU design weight, full pipeline variance methods run natively.

title: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
text: apply_step() internal S3 generic behind step. add adjustment, define step_*() constructor (inert) apply_step.<class>() method — nothing else changes.

title: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights

(1940). least squares adjustment sampled frequency table. Annals Mathematical Statistics, 11(4), 427–444. Lemaître, G., & Dufour, J. (1987). integrated method weighting persons families. what: Export weightflow weights to a survey design — as_svydesign
title: Export weightflow weights to a survey design — as_svydesign
text: as_svydesign() builds linearization (ultimate-cluster) design prepped recipe; as_svrepdesign() builds replicate-weights design bootstrap object, survey/srvyr standard errors include recipe's adjustments. require 'survey' package.

title: Export weightflow weights to a survey design — as_svydesign
code: as_svydesign(object, ids, strata = NULL, weight_name = ".weight", ...)  as_svrepdesign(boot, ...)

title: Export weightflow weights to a survey design — as_svydesign
text: object prepped recipe (as_svydesign) data frame weight design columns. ids, strata column names PSU stratum. weight_name name weight column. ... passed survey constructor. boot weightflow_boot object.

title: Export weightflow weights to a survey design — as_svydesign
text: survey.design / svyrep.design object.

what: Bootstrap estimate, standard error and confidence interval — bootstrap_estimate
title: Bootstrap estimate, standard error and confidence interval — bootstrap_estimate
text: Applies statistic point weights every replicate, summarises bootstrap variance \((1/B)\sum(\theta^*_b - \hat\theta)^2\).

title: Bootstrap estimate, standard error and confidence interval — bootstrap_estimate
code: bootstrap_estimate(boot, statistic, level = 0.95)  boot_total(boot, variable)  boot_mean(boot, variable)

title: Bootstrap estimate, standard error and confidence interval — bootstrap_estimate
text: boot weightflow_boot object. statistic function function(w, data) returning numeric scalar (vector) given weight vector data. level confidence level (normal) interval. variable name variable estimate.

title: Bootstrap estimate, standard error and confidence interval — bootstrap_estimate
text: data frame estimate, se, ci_lower, ci_upper.

what: Bootstrap replicate weights that re-apply the recipe — bootstrap_weights
title: Bootstrap replicate weights that re-apply the recipe — bootstrap_weights
text: Builds bootstrap replicate weights resampling primary sampling units (PSUs) replacement within strata re-running whole recipe replicate. every adjustment (nonresponse, calibration, ...) recomputed per replicate, resulting replicate weights propagate variability introduced weighting stage.

title: Bootstrap replicate weights that re-apply the recipe — bootstrap_weights
code: bootstrap_weights(   object,   replicates = 200L,   strata = NULL,   psu = NULL,   m = NULL,   seed = NULL,   progress = TRUE )

title: Bootstrap replicate weights that re-apply the recipe — bootstrap_weights
text: object weighting_spec (prepped one) holding recipe. replicates number bootstrap replicates. strata, psu column names stratum PSU. psu NULL unit PSU; strata NULL single stratum assumed. m PSUs drawn per stratum (default n - 1). seed optional RNG seed. progress print progress every 25 replicates.

title: Bootstrap replicate weights that re-apply the recipe — bootstrap_weights
text: object class weightflow_boot replicates matrix (units x replicates), point weights, design metadata.

title: Bootstrap replicate weights that re-apply the recipe — bootstrap_weights
text: multiplier Rao-Wu rescaling bootstrap: within stratum \(n\) PSUs, \(m\) PSUs drawn replacement (default \(m = n - 1\)) unit \(\) PSU \(k\) gets \(\lambda = 1 - \sqrt{m/(n-1)} + \sqrt{m/(n-1)}\,(n/m)\,t_k\), \(t_k\) number times PSU drawn.

title: Bootstrap replicate weights that re-apply the recipe — bootstrap_weights
code: spec <- weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_calibrate(method = "raking",                  margins = list(region = c(table(population$region)))) boot <- bootstrap_weights(spec, replicates = 50, strata = "region",                           psu = "psu", seed = 1) #>   bootstrap replicate 25/50 #>   bootstrap replicate 50/50 boot_total(boot, "responded") #>   estimate      se ci_lower ci_upper #> 1 2663.277 99.4537 2468.351 2858.203

what: Collect replicate weights into a data frame ready for srvyr — collect_replicate_weights
title: Collect replicate weights into a data frame ready for srvyr — collect_replicate_weights
text: Returns data point weight bootstrap replicate weights columns, can fed directly srvyr::as_survey_rep() (survey::svrepdesign()). Replicate columns full weights, use combined.weights = TRUE, scale = 1 / R, rscales = 1, mse = TRUE.

title: Collect replicate weights into a data frame ready for srvyr — collect_replicate_weights
code: collect_replicate_weights(   boot,   weight_name = ".weight",   prefix = "rep_",   drop_zero = TRUE )

title: Collect replicate weights into a data frame ready for srvyr — collect_replicate_weights
text: boot weightflow_boot object. weight_name name point-weight column add. prefix prefix replicate-weight columns (rep_1, rep_2, ...). drop_zero keep active units (point weight > 0).

title: Collect replicate weights into a data frame ready for srvyr — collect_replicate_weights
text: data frame: original columns, weight_name, one column per replicate. number replicates stored attribute "R".

title: Collect replicate weights into a data frame ready for srvyr — collect_replicate_weights
code: spec <- weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_calibrate(method = "raking",                  margins = list(region = c(table(population$region)))) boot <- bootstrap_weights(spec, replicates = 30, strata = "region",                           psu = "psu", seed = 1, progress = FALSE) df <- collect_replicate_weights(boot) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ srvyr::as_survey_rep(df, weights = .weight,                      repweights = dplyr::starts_with("rep_"),                      type = "bootstrap", combined.weights = TRUE,                      scale = 1 / attr(df, "R"), rscales = 1, mse = TRUE) } # }

what: Extract the data with the computed weights — collect_weights
title: Extract the data with the computed weights — collect_weights
text: Extract data computed weights

title: Extract the data with the computed weights — collect_weights
code: collect_weights(   object,   drop_zero = TRUE,   keep_intermediate = FALSE,   weight_name = ".weight" )

title: Extract the data with the computed weights — collect_weights
text: object prepped object (output prep()). drop_zero logical. TRUE, drops rows final weight 0 (ineligible / nonresponse). Default TRUE. keep_intermediate logical. TRUE, adds one column per stage. weight_name name final weight column. Default ".weight".

title: Extract the data with the computed weights — collect_weights
text: data.frame.

title: Extract the data with the computed weights — collect_weights
code: fitted <- weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class", by = "region") |>   prep() head(collect_weights(fitted)) #>    person_id household_id psu region sex age   pw unknown_elig responded income #> 1          8            3   1  North   F  28 12.5            0         1  52146 #> 5        195           92   5  North   M  18 12.5            0         1  28263 #> 6        198           94   6  North   M  40 12.5            0         1  33898 #> 8        248          117   7  North   F  32 12.5            0         1   8929 #> 9        249          117   7  North   F  50 12.5            0         1  33598 #> 10       250          117   7  North   F  58 12.5            0         1  25242 #>    employed  .weight #> 1         0 19.07051 #> 5         0 19.07051 #> 6         0 19.07051 #> 8         0 19.07051 #> 9         1 19.07051 #> 10        1 19.07051

what: Kish design effect from unequal weighting — design_effect
title: Kish design effect from unequal weighting — design_effect
text: deff = 1 + CV^2(w) = m * sum(w^2) / (sum(w))^2, active weights. effective sample size n_eff = m / deff.

title: Kish design effect from unequal weighting — design_effect
code: design_effect(w)

title: Kish design effect from unequal weighting — design_effect
text: w vector weights (zeros dropped).

title: Kish design effect from unequal weighting — design_effect
text: list deff, n_eff, cv n.

title: Kish design effect from unequal weighting — design_effect

what: Diagnostic plots for the weights — plot.prepped_weighting_spec
title: Diagnostic plots for the weights — plot.prepped_weighting_spec
text: Diagnostic plots weights

title: Diagnostic plots for the weights — plot.prepped_weighting_spec
code: # S3 method for class 'prepped_weighting_spec' plot(x, type = c("all", "factors", "summary"), ...)

title: Diagnostic plots for the weights — plot.prepped_weighting_spec
text: x prepped object (output prep()). type "" (default): per-step adjustment-factor histograms PLUS summary panel (final weights, cumulative factor, base vs final, deff stage), one grid. "factors": per-step factor histograms. "summary": summary panel. ... ignored.

title: Diagnostic plots for the weights — plot.prepped_weighting_spec
code: fitted <- weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class", by = "region") |>   prep() plot(fitted)

what: Example target population for weightflow — population
title: Example target population for weightflow — population
text: simulated population (sampling frame) individuals nested households primary sampling units (PSUs) within strata (regions), demographic auxiliaries two outcomes. Used illustrate calibration targets model calibration, validate weighted estimates. Generated data-raw/weightflow_data.R.

title: Example target population for weightflow — population
code: population

title: Example target population for weightflow — population
text: data frame one row per person: person_id individual identifier household_id household identifier (cluster) psu primary sampling unit (segment) within stratum region stratum: North, South, East West sex F M age age years (18-95) income annual income employed employment indicator (0/1)

title: Estimate the weighting cascade — prep
text: Walks steps order added, starting base weights. step multiplies current weight adjustment factor.

title: Estimate the weighting cascade — prep
code: prep(spec)

title: Estimate the weighting cascade — prep
text: spec weighting_spec.

title: Estimate the weighting cascade — prep
text: "prepped_weighting_spec" object.

title: Estimate the weighting cascade — prep
code: rec <- weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class", by = "region") prep(rec) #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. nonresponse (weighting class) #> Status  : estimated (prep) #>  #> Stage summary: #>                     stage n_active sum_wts cv_wts deff_kish n_eff #>                      base      467    4371  0.236     1.056   442 #>  stage_1_step_nonresponse      270    4371  0.144     1.021   265 #>  #> deff_kish = 1 + CV^2 (Kish design effect from unequal weighting); #> n_eff = n_active / deff_kish. Both worsen with each adjustment and #> improve with trimming. #>

what: Build a nice HTML report of the weighting recipe — report_weighting
title: Build a nice HTML report of the weighting recipe — report_weighting
text: Writes self-contained HTML file (dependencies, server) showing pipeline, parameters requested step, per-stage summary (n, sum, CV, Kish deff, effective n) per-step diagnostics, opens browser.

title: Build a nice HTML report of the weighting recipe — report_weighting
code: report_weighting(object, file = NULL, open = TRUE, plots = TRUE)

title: Build a nice HTML report of the weighting recipe — report_weighting
text: object prepped object (output prep()). file output path; NULL, temporary .html file. open logical; open file browser. plots logical; add per-step plots (weight -vs-scatter adjustment-factor histogram). Uses ggplot2 installed, else base graphics.

title: Build a nice HTML report of the weighting recipe — report_weighting
text: (invisibly) path HTML file.

title: Build a nice HTML report of the weighting recipe — report_weighting
code: fitted <- weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class", by = "region") |>   prep() if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ report_weighting(fitted)            # opens an HTML report in the browser } # }

title: Example survey sample (select-one-person, multistage) — sample_one
text: realistic multistage design (stratum -> PSU -> household, one selected person per household). Unknown-eligibility ineligible addresses appear single rows roster; resolved eligible households either reached (roster obtained) household nonresponse; reached households one person selected unequal within-household probability may may respond. Supports full household pipeline: household-level eligibility (cluster), dropping ineligibles, household person nonresponse, step_select_within. Generated data-raw/weightflow_data.R.

title: Example survey sample (select-one-person, multistage) — sample_one
code: sample_one

title: Example survey sample (select-one-person, multistage) — sample_one
text: data frame one row per sampled household (selected person, single placeholder row non-roster cases): person_id, household_id, psu identifiers region stratum sex, age selected person's attributes (NA non-roster rows) pw design base weight (product stage selection probabilities) status "eligible", "ineligible" "unknown" unknown_elig 1 eligibility unknown (roster) ineligible 1 address scope (roster) hh_responded 1 reached, 0 household nonresponse, NA non-eligible responded 1 selected person responded (NA non-roster rows) n_elig number eligible persons household (NA non-roster rows) p_within within-household selection probability selected person income, employed survey outcomes; NA unless person responded

title: Example survey sample (take-all roster) — sample_survey
text: stratified household sample drawn population every eligible person household kept (take-roster). Carries unequal design base weights, unknown-eligibility flag person-level response indicator; survey outcomes (income, employed) observed respondents. Generated data-raw/weightflow_data.R.

title: Example survey sample (take-all roster) — sample_survey
code: sample_survey

title: Example survey sample (take-all roster) — sample_survey
text: data frame one row per sampled person: person_id, household_id, psu identifiers region, sex, age frame auxiliaries, known units pw design base weight (inverse sampling fraction) unknown_elig 1 eligibility unknown responded 1 person responded income, employed survey outcomes; NA nonrespondents

what: Assert conditions on the weights at this point of the cascade — step_assert
title: Assert conditions on the weights at this point of the cascade — step_assert
text: checkpoint change weights; verifies conditions fails (error) warns met. Useful guard production pipeline (tidymodels-style tests inside recipe).

title: Assert conditions on the weights at this point of the cascade — step_assert
code: step_assert(   spec,   max_deff = NULL,   max_weight_ratio = NULL,   min_n_eff = NULL,   on_fail = c("error", "warning") )

title: Assert conditions on the weights at this point of the cascade — step_assert
text: spec weighting_spec. max_deff numeric NULL. Maximum acceptable Kish design effect. max_weight_ratio numeric NULL. Maximum allowed final/base weight ratio (per active unit). min_n_eff numeric NULL. Minimum acceptable effective sample size. on_fail "error" (stop cascade) "warning".

title: Assert conditions on the weights at this point of the cascade — step_assert
code: weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_assert(max_deff = 5, on_fail = "warning") |> prep() #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. assert (checkpoint) #> Status  : estimated (prep) #>  #> Stage summary: #>                stage n_active sum_wts cv_wts deff_kish n_eff #>                 base      467    4371  0.236     1.056   442 #>  stage_1_step_assert      467    4371  0.236     1.056   442 #>  #> deff_kish = 1 + CV^2 (Kish design effect from unequal weighting); #> n_eff = n_active / deff_kish. Both worsen with each adjustment and #> improve with trimming. #>

title: Calibration to population totals — step_calibrate
text: Calibration population totals

title: Calibration to population totals — step_calibrate
code: step_calibrate(   spec,   margins = NULL,   method = c("raking", "poststratify", "linear"),   formula = NULL,   totals = NULL,   cluster = NULL,   equal_within_cluster = FALSE,   calfun = c("linear", "logit"),   bounds = NULL,   maxit = 50L,   tol = 1e-06 )

title: Calibration to population totals — step_calibrate
text: spec weighting_spec. margins named list ("raking"/"poststratify"). element named numeric vector target totals per category. E.g.: list(sex = c(M = 5000, F = 5200), region = c(N = 3000, S = 7200)). method "raking" (IPF, categorical margins), "poststratify" (single categorical variable) "linear" (GREG / regression estimator; handles continuous categorical auxiliaries together). formula ("linear") auxiliary formula, e.g. ~ sex + income. Uses model.matrix; includes intercept unless write ~ 0 + ... totals ("linear") named numeric vector population totals, names matching model.matrix columns (including "(Intercept)" = N intercept). names match, error lists expected ones. cluster ("linear") name cluster id column (e.g. "household"), equal weights within cluster. equal_within_cluster ("linear") logical. TRUE, Lemaitre-Dufour (1987) integrative calibration: single weight per cluster. Requires cluster. Final weights equal within cluster provided incoming weight also uniform within cluster. calfun ("linear") distance function: "linear" (g = 1 + u) "logit" (bounded construction). "logit", bounds required. bounds ("linear") numeric c(L, U) L < 1 < U. Bounds calibration factor g (g-weights). "linear" truncates; "logit" enforced smoothly. Avoids extreme/negative weights without separate trimming step. maxit, tol convergence control raking bounded calibration.

title: Calibration to population totals — step_calibrate
code: # Raking to population margins weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class", by = "region") |>   step_calibrate(method = "raking",                  margins = list(sex    = c(table(population$sex)),                                 region = c(table(population$region)))) |>   prep() #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. nonresponse (weighting class) #>   2. calibration (raking) #> Status  : estimated (prep) #>  #> Stage summary: #>                     stage n_active sum_wts cv_wts deff_kish n_eff #>                      base      467    4371  0.236     1.056   442 #>  stage_1_step_nonresponse      270    4371  0.144     1.021   265 #>    stage_2_step_calibrate      270    4495  0.211     1.045   258 #>  #> deff_kish = 1 + CV^2 (Kish design effect from unequal weighting); #> n_eff = n_active / deff_kish. Both worsen with each adjustment and #> improve with trimming. #>

title: Drop ineligible (out-of-scope) units — step_drop_ineligible
text: Sets weight known-ineligible units zero leave cascade (excluded every later step collect_weights). redistribution done.

title: Drop ineligible (out-of-scope) units — step_drop_ineligible
code: step_drop_ineligible(spec, ineligible)

title: Drop ineligible (out-of-scope) units — step_drop_ineligible
text: spec weighting_spec. ineligible 0/1 dummy column (1 = ineligible) logical condition (unquoted) TRUE --scope units.

title: Drop ineligible (out-of-scope) units — step_drop_ineligible
text: Apply step_unknown_eligibility: ineligibles must present flagged unknown step, take part known-eligibility group receive share redistributed unknown weight. weight correctly discarded (represents ineligible share unknown units, scope).

title: Drop ineligible (out-of-scope) units — step_drop_ineligible
code: df <- transform(sample_survey,                 ineligible = as.integer(region == "West" & age > 90)) weighting_spec(df, base_weights = pw) |>   step_drop_ineligible(ineligible = ineligible) |>   prep() #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. drop ineligible #> Status  : estimated (prep) #>  #> Stage summary: #>                         stage n_active sum_wts cv_wts deff_kish n_eff #>                          base      467    4371  0.236     1.056   442 #>  stage_1_step_drop_ineligible      466    4364  0.236     1.055   442 #>  #> deff_kish = 1 + CV^2 (Kish design effect from unequal weighting); #> n_eff = n_active / deff_kish. Both worsen with each adjustment and #> improve with trimming. #>

title: Model calibration (model-assisted, Wu & Sitter 2001) — step_model_calibration
text: Fits working model study variable y, predicts population, calibrates weights sample total prediction equals population total (model-assisted efficiency). also calibrates X totals (consistency auxiliary controls).

title: Model calibration (model-assisted, Wu & Sitter 2001) — step_model_calibration
code: step_model_calibration(   spec,   x_formula,   models,   population,   cluster = NULL,   equal_within_cluster = FALSE )

title: Model calibration (model-assisted, Wu & Sitter 2001) — step_model_calibration
text: spec weighting_spec. x_formula formula consistency auxiliaries, e.g. ~ sex + region. models named list models created y_model(). names label prediction constraints. population population data.frame auxiliary predictor columns (y variables needed; predicted). cluster name cluster id column (e.g. "household"), equal weights within cluster. equal_within_cluster logical. TRUE, integrative calibration: single weight per cluster. Requires cluster incoming weight uniform within cluster.

title: Model calibration (model-assisted, Wu & Sitter 2001) — step_model_calibration
text: Requires COMPLETE auxiliary information: data.frame population x_formula columns model predictors whole population (reference frame/census).

title: Model calibration (model-assisted, Wu & Sitter 2001) — step_model_calibration
code: weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class", by = "region") |>   step_model_calibration(     x_formula  = ~ sex + region,     models     = list(income = y_model(income ~ age + sex, engine = "glm")),     population = population) |>   prep() #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. nonresponse (weighting class) #>   2. model calibration (1 y variables) #> Status  : estimated (prep) #>  #> Stage summary: #>                           stage n_active sum_wts cv_wts deff_kish n_eff #>                            base      467    4371  0.236     1.056   442 #>        stage_1_step_nonresponse      270    4371  0.144     1.021   265 #>  stage_2_step_model_calibration      270    4495  0.212     1.045   258 #>  #> deff_kish = 1 + CV^2 (Kish design effect from unequal weighting); #> n_eff = n_active / deff_kish. Both worsen with each adjustment and #> improve with trimming. #>

title: Nonresponse adjustment — step_nonresponse
text: Nonresponse adjustment

title: Nonresponse adjustment — step_nonresponse
code: step_nonresponse(   spec,   respondent,   method = c("weighting_class", "propensity"),   by = NULL,   formula = NULL,   engine = c("logit", "tree", "forest"),   num_classes = 5L,   cluster = NULL )

title: Nonresponse adjustment — step_nonresponse
text: spec weighting_spec. respondent 0/1 dummy column (1 = responded) logical condition (unquoted) TRUE respondents. Eligible cases respondents treated nonresponse. method "weighting_class" (cells) "propensity" (predictive model). character. Adjustment cells method = "weighting_class". formula predictor formula (right-hand side ), e.g. ~ age + region, used method = "propensity". engine engine estimate propensity method = "propensity": "logit" (logistic regression, base R), "tree" (CART via package 'rpart') "forest" (random forest via package 'ranger'). 'rpart' 'ranger' optional: needed pick engine. num_classes integer NULL. Controls propensities used: integer forms many propensity classes (cell adjustment within class); NULL applies direct factor 1/p unit. cluster character NULL. given, adjustment done cluster (e.g. household) level whole-household nonresponse: household counts (uniform) weight; "weighting_class" redistribution responding nonresponding households within cells, "propensity" model fitted one row per household (household auxiliaries), predicting household response. resulting factor assigned every member; nonresponding households go zero. always, active units (weight > 0) take part, units already dropped (unknown eligibility, ineligible) excluded automatically.

title: Nonresponse adjustment — step_nonresponse
code: weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class",                    by = "region") #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. nonresponse (weighting class) #> Status  : not estimated #>   # household-level nonresponse (whole household responds or not) weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class",                    by = "region", cluster = "household_id") |>   prep() #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. nonresponse (weighting class, by household_id) #> Status  : estimated (prep) #>  #> Stage summary: #>                     stage n_active sum_wts cv_wts deff_kish n_eff #>                      base      467    4371  0.236     1.056   442 #>  stage_1_step_nonresponse      111    3047  0.253     1.064   104 #>  #> deff_kish = 1 + CV^2 (Kish design effect from unequal weighting); #> n_eff = n_active / deff_kish. Both worsen with each adjustment and #> improve with trimming. #>

title: Rescale (normalize) the weights — step_rescale
text: Rescale (normalize) weights

title: Rescale (normalize) the weights — step_rescale
code: step_rescale(spec, to = c("n", "total"), total = NULL, by = NULL)

title: Rescale (normalize) the weights — step_rescale
text: spec weighting_spec. "n" (weights sum number active units, .e. mean weight 1) "total" (weights sum total). total numeric. Target sum = "total". character. Rescale within groups (optional). = "n", group sums active count.

title: Rescale (normalize) the weights — step_rescale
code: weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_rescale(to = "n") |> prep() #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. rescale (to n) #> Status  : estimated (prep) #>  #> Stage summary: #>                 stage n_active sum_wts cv_wts deff_kish n_eff #>                  base      467    4371  0.236     1.056   442 #>  stage_1_step_rescale      467     467  0.236     1.056   442 #>  #> deff_kish = 1 + CV^2 (Kish design effect from unequal weighting); #> n_eff = n_active / deff_kish. Both worsen with each adjustment and #> improve with trimming. #>

title: Round the final weights — step_round
text: Optional step, typically last one (calibration). Simple rounding ("nearest") slightly breaks calibrated totals; "preserve_total" uses largest-remainder method keep exact total.

title: Round the final weights — step_round
code: step_round(spec, digits = 0L, method = c("nearest", "preserve_total"))

title: Round the final weights — step_round
text: spec weighting_spec. digits integer. Decimals keep (0 = integers). method "nearest" (simple rounding) "preserve_total" (keeps sum weights). Note: "preserve_total" can break equality weights within cluster; need integer equal weights per household, use "nearest".

title: Round the final weights — step_round
code: weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_round(digits = 0) |> prep() #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. rounding (nearest, 0 decimals) #> Status  : estimated (prep) #>  #> Stage summary: #>               stage n_active sum_wts cv_wts deff_kish n_eff #>                base      467    4371  0.236     1.056   442 #>  stage_1_step_round      467    4323  0.211     1.045   447 #>  #> deff_kish = 1 + CV^2 (Kish design effect from unequal weighting); #> n_eff = n_active / deff_kish. Both worsen with each adjustment and #> improve with trimming. #>

title: Within-household selection adjustment — step_select_within
text: one (subsample) eligible persons selected within household, selected person represents eligible persons, weight multiplied inverse within-household selection probability. Apply (household-level) eligibility adjustment nonresponse adjustment.

title: Within-household selection adjustment — step_select_within
code: step_select_within(spec, prob = NULL, n_eligible = NULL)

title: Within-household selection adjustment — step_select_within
text: spec weighting_spec. prob unquoted column within-household selection probability selected person (need 1/n_eligible). weight multiplied 1/prob. n_eligible unquoted column number eligible persons household, simple random selection one person. weight multiplied n_eligible (equivalent prob = 1/n_eligible).

title: Within-household selection adjustment — step_select_within
code: # simple random selection of one eligible person per household df <- transform(sample_survey,                 n_elig = ave(person_id, household_id, FUN = length)) weighting_spec(df, base_weights = pw) |>   step_select_within(n_eligible = n_elig) #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. within-household selection #> Status  : not estimated #>

title: Trim extreme weights — step_trim
text: Caps weights limit , optionally, redistributes excess among others preserve weighted total (Potter 1988, 1990; Liu et al. 2004). Optional step can inserted anywhere recipe, even several times. Operates CURRENT weights point cascade.

title: Trim extreme weights — step_trim
code: step_trim(   spec,   max_ratio,   min_ratio = NULL,   reference = c("base", "median", "value"),   redistribute = TRUE,   by = NULL,   maxit = 50L )

title: Trim extreme weights — step_trim
text: spec weighting_spec. max_ratio number. Upper cap. meaning depends reference. E.g. reference = "base" max_ratio = 4, weight may exceed 4 times design weight. min_ratio number NULL. Lower floor (units max_ratio). reference "base" (multiple unit's base weight), "median" (multiple median current weights) "value" (absolute weight value). redistribute logical. TRUE, redistributes trimmed excess among uncapped weights preserve total (iterating). calibrate afterwards can use FALSE: calibration restores totals. character. Groups within redistribute (optional). maxit integer. Maximum cap+redistribution iterations.

title: Trim extreme weights — step_trim
text: standard threshold: max_ratio analyst decision, bias-variance trade-. Use Kish's design effect (see summary) judge whether trimming worth .

title: Trim extreme weights — step_trim
code: weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_trim(max_ratio = 3, reference = "base") #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. trimming (base, cap 3) #> Status  : not estimated #>

title: Automatic weight trimming (survey-style) — step_trim_weights
text: Caps weights [lower, upper] redistributes change among untrimmed units preserve total, mirroring survey::trimWeights(). default weight may fall 1, upper cap set automatic empirical rule (Tukey far-fence: Q3 + 3*IQR).

title: Automatic weight trimming (survey-style) — step_trim_weights
code: step_trim_weights(spec, lower = 1, upper = NULL, strict = TRUE, maxit = 50L)

title: Automatic weight trimming (survey-style) — step_trim_weights
text: spec weighting_spec. lower numeric. Lower floor (default 1: weight 1). upper numeric NULL. Upper cap. NULL, automatic rule Q3 + 3*IQR active weights. strict logical. TRUE (default), iterate cap+redistribution weight outside [lower, upper] (like survey's strict = TRUE). FALSE, single pass (redistribution may push weights slightly past cap). maxit integer. Maximum iterations strict = TRUE.

title: Automatic weight trimming (survey-style) — step_trim_weights
code: weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class", by = "region") |>   step_trim_weights(lower = 1, strict = TRUE) |> prep() #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. nonresponse (weighting class) #>   2. auto weight trimming #> Status  : estimated (prep) #>  #> Stage summary: #>                      stage n_active sum_wts cv_wts deff_kish n_eff #>                       base      467    4371  0.236     1.056   442 #>   stage_1_step_nonresponse      270    4371  0.144     1.021   265 #>  stage_2_step_trim_weights      270    4371  0.144     1.021   265 #>  #> deff_kish = 1 + CV^2 (Kish design effect from unequal weighting); #> n_eff = n_active / deff_kish. Both worsen with each adjustment and #> improve with trimming. #>

title: Unknown-eligibility adjustment — step_unknown_eligibility
text: Redistributes weight unknown-eligibility cases among known-eligibility cases, within cells defined .

title: Unknown-eligibility adjustment — step_unknown_eligibility
code: step_unknown_eligibility(spec, unknown, by = NULL, cluster = NULL)

title: Unknown-eligibility adjustment — step_unknown_eligibility
text: spec weighting_spec. unknown 0/1 dummy column (1 = eligibility unknown) logical condition (unquoted) TRUE unknown-eligibility cases. Evaluated data. character. Variables defining adjustment cells (optional). cluster character. Cluster (e.g. household) id column. given, redistribution done cluster level: cluster counts (uniform) weight, weight unknown-eligibility clusters redistributed among known ones, adjusted weight assigned every member. Use unknown-eligibility units roster (one row per address) resolved units expanded person.

title: Unknown-eligibility adjustment — step_unknown_eligibility
code: weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_unknown_eligibility(unknown = unknown_elig, by = "region") #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. unknown eligibility #> Status  : not estimated #>   # household-level redistribution (unknown units without roster) weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_unknown_eligibility(unknown = unknown_elig, by = "region",                            cluster = "household_id") #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. unknown eligibility (by household_id) #> Status  : not estimated #>

title: Detailed per-step diagnostics — summary.prepped_weighting_spec
text: Detailed per-step diagnostics

title: Detailed per-step diagnostics — summary.prepped_weighting_spec
code: # S3 method for class 'prepped_weighting_spec' summary(object, ...)

title: Detailed per-step diagnostics — summary.prepped_weighting_spec
text: object prepped object (output prep()). ... ignored.

title: Detailed per-step diagnostics — summary.prepped_weighting_spec

title: Detailed per-step diagnostics — summary.prepped_weighting_spec
code: fitted <- weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class", by = "region") |>   prep() summary(fitted) #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : #>   1. nonresponse (weighting class) #> Status  : estimated (prep) #>  #> Stage summary: #>                     stage n_active sum_wts cv_wts deff_kish n_eff #>                      base      467    4371  0.236     1.056   442 #>  stage_1_step_nonresponse      270    4371  0.144     1.021   265 #>  #> deff_kish = 1 + CV^2 (Kish design effect from unequal weighting); #> n_eff = n_active / deff_kish. Both worsen with each adjustment and #> improve with trimming. #>  #> --- Step 1: nonresponse (weighting class) --- #>   cell n_respondents n_nonresponse   factor #>   East            52            44 1.846154 #>  North            78            41 1.525641 #>  South            72            49 1.680556 #>   West            68            63 1.926471 #> Kish deff: 1.056 -> 1.021   |   n_eff: 442 -> 265 #>

title: Per-unit adjustment factors table — weight_factors
text: Returns data.frame weight stage factor step (stage weight / previous-stage weight), handy custom plots.

title: Per-unit adjustment factors table — weight_factors
code: weight_factors(object)

title: Per-unit adjustment factors table — weight_factors
text: object prepped object (output prep()).

title: Per-unit adjustment factors table — weight_factors
text: data.frame one weight column per stage one factor per step.

title: Per-unit adjustment factors table — weight_factors
code: fitted <- weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class", by = "region") |>   prep() head(weight_factors(fitted)) #>   base stage_1_step_nonresponse factor_stage_1_step_nonresponse #> 1 12.5                 19.07051                        1.525641 #> 2 12.5                  0.00000                        0.000000 #> 3 12.5                  0.00000                        0.000000 #> 4 12.5                  0.00000                        0.000000 #> 5 12.5                 19.07051                        1.525641 #> 6 12.5                 19.07051                        1.525641

title: weightflow: declarative survey weighting — weightflow-package
text: Build survey weights design base weights chaining hierarchical adjustments (unknown eligibility, nonresponse, trimming, calibration, rounding, rescaling, assertions) declarative, pipeable, tidymodels-style API. Computes weights ; variance/inference, export weights use 'survey' package.

title: weightflow: declarative survey weighting — weightflow-package
text: Start weighting_spec(), add step_*() adjustments, estimate cascade prep(), extract weights collect_weights(). Inspect summary(), plot() report_weighting().

title: weightflow: declarative survey weighting — weightflow-package
text: Maintainer: Juan Pablo Ferreira juanpablo.ferreira@fcea.edu.uy Authors: Juan Pablo Ferreira juanpablo.ferreira@fcea.edu.uy

title: Start a weighting specification — weighting_spec
text: Creates inert recipe object. Nothing computed prep() called.

title: Start a weighting specification — weighting_spec
code: weighting_spec(data, base_weights)

title: Start a weighting specification — weighting_spec
text: data data.frame sample units (one row per case). base_weights unquoted name design base-weight column.

title: Start a weighting specification — weighting_spec
text: object class "weighting_spec".

title: Start a weighting specification — weighting_spec
code: rec <- weighting_spec(sample_survey, base_weights = pw) rec #>  #> == Weighting specification (weightflow) == #> Data    : 467 cases #> Base wts: pw #> Steps   : (none yet) #> Status  : not estimated #>

code: y_model(formula, engine = c("glm", "tree", "forest"), family = NULL)

title: Specify a working model for a study variable y — y_model
text: formula full formula, e.g. income ~ sex + age_g. engine "glm", "tree" (rpart) "forest" (ranger). family engine = "glm": "gaussian", "binomial" "poisson". tree/forest, regression vs classification inferred y.

title: Specify a working model for a study variable y — y_model
text: model specification list.

title: Specify a working model for a study variable y — y_model
code: y_model(income ~ age + sex, engine = "glm") #> $formula #> income ~ age + sex #> <environment: 0x5582c775ebd8> #>  #> $engine #> [1] "glm" #>  #> $family #> NULL #>

title: weightflow 0.1.0
text: First release. dependency-free, pipeable API compute survey weights design base weights chain hierarchical adjustment stages. Build recipe lazily, estimate prep(), extract weights collect_weights().

title: weightflow 0.1.0
text: step_unknown_eligibility() — redistribute weight unknown-eligibility cases known ones (person- household-level via cluster). step_drop_ineligible() — zero --scope units. step_select_within() — within-household selection (unequal prob equal n_eligible). step_nonresponse() — weighting-class propensity adjustment, person household level (cluster). step_calibrate() — raking, post-stratification linear/GREG calibration, bounded (Deville-Särndal) integrative cluster options. step_model_calibration() — Wu-Sitter model calibration. step_trim(), step_trim_weights(), step_round(), step_rescale() — trimming, rounding rescaling. step_assert() — quality checkpoint (deff, weight ratio, effective n).

title: weightflow 0.1.0
text: summary(), plot() weight_factors() per-stage diagnostics. design_effect() Kish design effect effective sample size. report_weighting() builds self-contained HTML report pipeline diagram, variables used, per-stage summaries per-step visuals.

title: weightflow 0.1.0
text: Bundled example datasets population, sample_survey (take-roster) sample_one (multistage select-one design). package produces weights ; variance estimation, export final weights survey package.
